proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_005` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_005` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 36.1% for feature_005=1 (n=20076) versus 15.9% for feature_005=0 (n=29924); risk difference 0.2024, z=50.67, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_034` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_034` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 15.4% for feature_034=1 versus 34.6% for feature_034=0; risk difference -0.1925, z=-50.07, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_009` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_009` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.0% for feature_009=1 versus 26.0% for feature_009=0; risk difference -0.1005, z=-23.56, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_065` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_065` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 15.0% for feature_065=1 versus 25.0% for feature_065=0; risk difference -0.1005, z=-18.47, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_055` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_055` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 17.6% for feature_055=1 versus 24.2% for feature_055=0; risk difference -0.0662, z=-6.66, p=2.71e-11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with high `feature_003` values (above the cohort median 15.86) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients at or below the median.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.2% above median feature_003 versus 29.8% at/below median; risk difference -0.1161, z=-30.67, p=<1e-300. The top quartile test at >41.28 was similar: risk difference -0.1169, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_023` = 2 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_023` = 0, consistent with a decreasing response gradient across `feature_023` levels 0, 1, and 2.

analyses.method: stratified response rates and two-proportion z-test
analyses.result_summary: Response by feature_023 level was 28.2% at 0 (n=17592), 22.8% at 1 (n=24971), and 18.2% at 2 (n=7437); level 2 vs 0 risk difference -0.1005, z=-17.91, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_063` above the upper quartile (6.4) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients at or below that quartile.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 21.0% for feature_063>6.4 versus 25.0% for feature_063<=6.4; risk difference -0.0403, z=-9.36, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_054` above the upper quartile (6.98) have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients at or below that quartile.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.1% for feature_054>6.98 versus 24.7% for feature_054<=6.98; risk difference -0.0257, z=-5.93, p=2.96e-9.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027` above the upper quartile (4.1) have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients at or below that quartile.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 25.1% for feature_027>4.1 versus 23.7% for feature_027<=4.1; risk difference 0.0137, z=3.06, p=2.21e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for all nonconstant features, `feature_005` remains independently associated with a higher `objective_response` rate.
After adjustment for all nonconstant features, `feature_034` remains independently associated with a lower `objective_response` rate.
After adjustment for all nonconstant features, `feature_009` remains independently associated with a lower `objective_response` rate.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature linear probability model: adjusted coefficient for feature_005 was 0.2025 on the response-rate scale (binary 1 vs 0), z=55.65, p=<1e-300.
All-feature linear probability model: adjusted coefficient for feature_034 was -0.1881 on the response-rate scale (binary 1 vs 0), z=-50.05, p=<1e-300.
All-feature linear probability model: adjusted coefficient for feature_009 was -0.1000 on the response-rate scale (binary 1 vs 0), z=-22.46, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for all nonconstant features, `feature_065` remains independently associated with a lower `objective_response` rate.
After adjustment for all nonconstant features, a one-standard-deviation increase in `feature_003` remains independently associated with a lower `objective_response` rate.
After adjustment for all nonconstant features, high `feature_027` remains independently associated with a higher `objective_response` rate.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature linear probability model: adjusted coefficient for feature_065 was -0.1021 on the response-rate scale (binary 1 vs 0), z=-17.15, p=<1e-300.
All-feature linear probability model: adjusted coefficient for one SD higher feature_003 was -0.0085, z=-4.55, p=5.30e-6.
All-feature linear probability model: adjusted coefficient for one SD higher feature_027 was 0.0098, z=5.51, p=3.57e-8.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` = 1 and `objective_response` is much smaller when `feature_034` = 1 than when `feature_034` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Feature_005 risk difference was 0.4403 when feature_034=0 and 0.0077 when feature_034=1; interaction difference-in-differences -0.4326, z=-57.51, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` = 1 and `objective_response` is much smaller when `feature_009` = 1 than when `feature_009` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Feature_005 risk difference was 0.2510 when feature_009=0 and 0.0085 when feature_009=1; interaction difference-in-differences -0.2425, z=-27.66, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` = 1 and `objective_response` is much smaller when `feature_065` = 1 than when `feature_065` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Feature_005 risk difference was 0.2246 when feature_065=0 and 0.0063 when feature_065=1; interaction difference-in-differences -0.2183, z=-19.57, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` = 1 and `objective_response` is much smaller when `feature_055` = 1 than when `feature_055` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Feature_005 risk difference was 0.2092 when feature_055=0 and -0.0221 when feature_055=1; interaction difference-in-differences -0.2312, z=-11.42, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `feature_034` = 0, `feature_009` = 0, `feature_065` = 0, and `feature_055` = 0, `feature_005` = 1 is associated with a much higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_005` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: When all four negative binary markers were absent, response was 79.8% for feature_005=1 (n=6325) versus 17.2% for feature_005=0 (n=9356); risk difference 0.6257, z=98.04, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with at least one of `feature_034` = 1, `feature_009` = 1, `feature_065` = 1, or `feature_055` = 1, `feature_005` = 1 has little or no increase in `objective_response` rate compared with `feature_005` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: With any of the four negative binary markers present, response was 16.1% for feature_005=1 versus 15.3% for feature_005=0; risk difference 0.0077, z=1.92, p=5.54e-2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within patients with `feature_005` = 1, having any of `feature_034` = 1, `feature_009` = 1, `feature_065` = 1, or `feature_055` = 1 is associated with a sharply lower `objective_response` rate than having none of these four markers.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_005=1 patients, response was 16.1% with any negative marker versus 79.8% with none; risk difference -0.6371, z=-107.22, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` = 1 and `objective_response` is attenuated when `feature_003` is above the cohort median (15.86) compared with when `feature_003` is at or below the median.

analyses.result_summary: Feature_005 risk difference was 0.3131 when feature_003<=15.86 and 0.0913 when feature_003>15.86; interaction difference-in-differences -0.2219, z=-28.47, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative associations of `feature_034` = 1 and `feature_009` = 1 with `objective_response` overlap, so their joint decrement is less negative than the sum of their separate decrements on the response-rate scale.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_034-by-feature_009 response-rate difference-in-differences was 0.2210, z=25.75, p=<1e-300, supporting overlapping rather than additive negative effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative associations of `feature_034` = 1 and `feature_065` = 1 with `objective_response` overlap, so their joint decrement is less negative than the sum of their separate decrements on the response-rate scale.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_034-by-feature_065 response-rate difference-in-differences was 0.2098, z=19.17, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative associations of `feature_009` = 1 and `feature_065` = 1 with `objective_response` overlap, so their joint decrement is less negative than the sum of their separate decrements on the response-rate scale.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_009-by-feature_065 response-rate difference-in-differences was 0.1263, z=9.21, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The subgroup with `feature_005` = 1, all four negative binary markers absent, and `feature_003` at or below the median (15.86) has a higher `objective_response` rate than all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: The refined high-response subgroup had response 80.9% (n=4938) versus 17.8% in all other patients; risk difference 0.6309, z=107.32, p=<1e-300. Within feature_005=1/all-four-marker-absent patients, low feature_003 exceeded high feature_003 by 0.0504.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_039` above the cohort median (65) have a different, specifically lower, `objective_response` rate than patients at or below the median.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 23.9% for feature_039>65 versus 24.1% for feature_039<=65; risk difference -0.0016, z=-0.42, p=6.73e-1. This directional hypothesis was not supported.
